subject: Add package dist file
commit_message:
Add package dist file

It's related with pbr bug (https://bugs.launchpad.net/pbr/+bug/1530867) so packages with submodules cannot be installed using `python setup.py install` command (which is called on `pip install <url>).
Package can be installed then using `pip install <dist_file_url_path>` command.

Issue-ID: TEST-293
Signed-off-by: Michal Jagiello <michal.jagiello@t-mobile.pl>
Change-Id: I605d0dae355e05e7022ceecf07f5a741c8df475e

Patch Set 3:

I am not 100% sure to follow

due to the pbr bug, there is a workaround consisting in replacing the 
python setup.py install by 
 python setup.py develop 
or python setup.py sdist followed by pip install dist/your_package.tar.gz

to build the package we need to select the second option so a patch in integration/xtesting is needed 
but for the pythonsdk-tests repo, why do you push a dist package that shall be changed at every commit.
If the workarounds are OK locally, why do you want to push a dist package...

I may miss something here...
